key_decisions.detail: Up to £104bn for 2025-30 against company requests of £112bn, a 7% overall cost challenge
0.5% per annum applied to all expenditure allowances
Econometric models to benchmark efficient base costs across companies with company-specific adjustments for unmodelled costs
Combined econometric modelling, deep dives for large schemes, and unit cost benchmarking

key_numbers.label: Sector total company requests
key_numbers.context: Total company requests across the sector

key_numbers.label: Overall cost challenge
key_numbers.context: Reduction from company requests to Ofwat allowances

glossary_terms.definition: Total expenditure — the sum of capital expenditure (capex) and operating expenditure (opex)
Expenditure to maintain current service levels and meet ongoing regulatory requirements; benchmarked using econometric models
Expenditure to deliver new or improved service levels above baseline, including environmental improvements
Annual efficiency improvement applied to expenditure allowances, set at 0.5% p.a. for AMP8
Adjustments for input price inflation differing from CPIH
Mechanism that shares cost outperformance or underperformance between companies and customers
Upper quartile — benchmark level used for base cost efficiency assessment
Adjustment applied to bring less efficient companies towards the efficiency frontier
